name: @dudousxd/nestjs-context
description: Shared AsyncLocalStorage context for NestJS — carries user/tenant/traceId across the request and the ecosystem.
author: Davi Carvalho <davi@goflip.ai>
//sideEffects: NOT false: tokens.ts registers process-global symbols via Symbol.for() AT IMPORT TIME — the cross-package DI token channel other ecosystem libs reference. context.ts holds the module-level singleton (AsyncLocalStorage + cross-boundary config) that non-DI entrypoints (queue/durable workers) rely on being one shared instance. A bundler told sideEffects:false could tree-shake these initializers, breaking token identity / singleton sharing. The array lists exactly those modules (src + published dist paths); pure modules (index, accessor, middleware, etc.) stay tree-shakeable. Decorator/metadata-bearing classes are reached through value imports, so they are never dropped.
